item.title: LTD PHX-200DX Red Burst (LPHX200DXREDB) — $499
item.description:
$499 · Brand New · Musician Madness

LTD’s DX range allows for musicians to get access to a high-quality guitar at a price far less than its impressive features would imply. With the LTD Phoenix-200DX, you get the flowing curves of the Phoenix shape with an exciting Red Burst finish on its flamed maple top. Built with bolt-on construction at 25.5” scale, this guitar features a comfortable poplar body and a smooth, thin U-shaped roasted maple neck with rosewood fingerboard and 22 extra-jumbo frets. Components on the Phoenix-200DX include black hardware, a TOM-style bridge and tailpiece, LTD tuners, and a set of acclaimed ESP LH-150 passive humbucking pickups with a push-pull control to split the bridge pickup into single coil mode.

Construction Bolt-On Scale 25.5" Body Poplar Top Flamed Maple Neck Roasted Maple Fingerboard Rosewood Fingerboard Radius 350mm Finish Red Burst Nut Width 42mm Nut Type Molded Neck Contour Thin U Frets/Type22 XJ Hardware Color Black Strap Button Standard Tuners LTD Bridge TOM & Tailpiece Bridge PUESP LH-150B Neck PUESP LH-150N Electronics Layout Volume/Tone(Push/Pull)/Toggle Switch Strings D'Addario XL110 (.010/.013/.017/.026/.036/.046) Case Case Included N

item.title: Peavey T-40 Case Good Latches Clean Interior — $159
item.description:
$159 · Good · Six String Sing Music

For sale is an original vintage Peavey T-40 bass molded hardshell case, early production (1978–1981).

These first-generation cases have the dark reddish-brown plush interior, rectangular metal latches, and the classic molded Peavey logo on the shell. Top Liner sags a little and the pin that holds the lid up can pop out. Very slight smoke odor.


Fits: Peavey T-40 bass

Interior: Original brown plush

Exterior: Molded ABS with ribbed reinforcement

Hardware: All original latches and hinges

Condition:

– Structurally solid

– Interior clean for its age

– Normal scuffs and wear from use

– All hardware functions properly (if true—adjust as needed)


These early cases are getting hard to find, especially in fully usable condition. Perfect for collectors, restorers, or anyone wanting an authentic original case for their T-40.

item.description:

item.title: Schecter P-5 Electric Bass in 3 Tone Sunburst — $999
item.description:
$999 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Schecter P-5 Electric Bass in 3 Tone Sunburst

The Schecter P-5 delivers a comfortable, fast playing experience with its thin "C" neck profile and 35" extended scale that accommodates five-string bass work with authority. The 3 Tone Sunburst finish presents a classic aesthetic while the bolt-on construction ensures solid sustain and resonance. This is a versatile instrument designed for players who demand responsive feel and dependable electronics.

Playability & Feel

The thin "C" neck shape at the 1st fret (.787") transitions smoothly through the 12th fret (.866"), providing consistent comfort across the entire playing range. Combined with the 16" fretboard radius and 21 Narrow Extra Jumbo frets, the P-5 offers both precision and speed; the Graph Tech XL Ivory Tusq nut at 1.771" wide maintains clarity and sustain across all five strings, while offset/reverse dot inlays ensure quick navigation in low-light situations.

Tone & Electronics

The Schecter USA MonsterTone-P pickup paired with simple volume and tone controls delivers focused, punchy bass character without unnecessary complexity. The Schecter High Mass Bridge with string-through construction maximizes sustain and harmonic content, while Schecter Light-Weight tuners keep head weight minimal and maintain stable tuning across extended sessions.

Key Features
  • Body Material: Alder with bolt-on construction
  • Neck Material: Maple with Rosewood fretboard
  • Finish: 3 Tone Sunburst
  • Scale: 35" (889mm)
  • Neck Shape: Thin "C"
  • Frets: 21 Narrow Extra Jumbo with offset/reverse dot inlays
  • Nut: Graph Tech XL Ivory Tusq, 1.771" width
  • Pickup: Schecter USA MonsterTone-P
  • Bridge: Schecter High Mass Bass String Thru (or Top Load)
  • Tuners: Schecter Light-Weight
  • Controls: Volume and Tone with metal knurled knobs
  • Truss Rod: 2-Way adjustable with heel access spoke wheel

The Schecter P-5 combines refined hardware, a responsive neck, and proven pickup electronics into a five-string bass that performs across genres with minimal fuss.

item.title: Schecter Michael Anthony MA-4 Electric Bass Gloss Natural — $1,399
item.description:
$1,399 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Schecter Michael Anthony MA-4 Electric Bass Gloss Natural

The Michael Anthony MA-4 delivers a comfortable, fast playing experience with its thin "C"-shaped neck and 34" scale, designed for extended reach and articulate note definition. The Koa top mounted on a Swamp Ash body creates a visually striking instrument while contributing warmth and resonance to the overall tone. This bass bridges professional craftsmanship with approachable playability, making it a solid choice for players seeking quality construction without compromise.

Playability & Feel

The thin "C" neck shape and carefully calculated neck thickness—measuring .787" at the 1st fret and .866" at the 12th fret—provide responsive hand positioning across the full 21 extra-jumbo frets. A 12" fretboard radius and Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut (1.496" wide) ensure smooth note articulation and comfortable string spacing for four-string playing.

Tone & Electronics

The dual Schecter USA MonsterTone pickups—a J-style bridge and P-style neck configuration—deliver passive tone shaping through a simple volume/tone/3-way switch control setup. This straightforward electronics layout allows the pickups to express the natural resonance of the Koa and Swamp Ash construction without active preamp coloration.

Key Features
  • Body: Swamp Ash with Koa top; bolt-on construction
  • Neck: Maple/Walnut multi-ply with thin "C" shape
  • Fretboard: Rosewood with white pearloid dot inlays
  • Scale: 34" (863.6 mm)
  • Frets: 21 extra-jumbo; 12" fretboard radius
  • Nut: Graph Tech XL Black Tusq; 1.496" width
  • Pickups: Schecter USA MonsterTone-J (bridge) and MonsterTone-P (neck)
  • Controls: Volume/tone/3-way switch (passive)
  • Hardware: WSC KG400 tuners; WSC THD4 bass bridge; metal knurled knobs
  • Black hardware throughout
  • Includes Ernie Ball 4-string super-long strings (.045–.105)
  • Weight: Approximately 8.5 lbs

The MA-4 combines thoughtful ergonomics with quality hardware and passive pickup tone, making it a capable instrument for working bassists and serious hobbyists.

item.title: Schecter Riot-5 Electric Bass Satin Inferno Burst B-Stock 2751 — $959.20
item.description:
$959.20 · B-Stock · LV Guitars

Schecter Riot-5 Electric Bass Satin Inferno Burst B-Stock

The Riot-5 delivers a comfortable, fast playing experience with its thin "C" neck contour and 35" extended scale, making it equally at home in tight technical passages or extended lower register work. Built on a swamp ash body with burl maple top, this five-string carries a sophisticated visual presence in satin inferno burst finish. This B-stock example is in excellent overall condition with minor cosmetic wear, making it an outstanding value.

Playability & Construction

The bolt-on maple/walnut multi-ply neck paired with an ebony fingerboard creates a responsive, stable platform across all registers. Twenty-four X-Jumbo frets and a 16-inch fretboard radius support smooth lateral movement and precise intonation, while the 45mm Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut ensures reliable string spacing and tonal clarity from the open position.

Tone & Electronics

EMG's 40JX-CS bridge and 40PX-CS neck pickups deliver punchy, articulate output with excellent note definition across the five-string range. The EMG-B30 3-band active EQ paired with master volume and blend controls provides comprehensive shaping capability, from scooped low-end punch to aggressive midrange cut, all powered by a 9-volt clip-in battery system.

Key Features
  • Bolt-on construction with swamp ash body and burl maple top
  • Maple/walnut multi-ply neck with ebony fingerboard
  • 35-inch extended scale length
  • 24 X-Jumbo frets with 16-inch fretboard radius
  • Thin "C" neck contour (.787" at 1st fret, .866" at 12th fret)
  • 45mm Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut
  • EMG 40JX-CS bridge and 40PX-CS neck pickups
  • EMG-B30 3-band active EQ with master volume and blend controls
  • Black hardware with Schecter tuners and string-thru bridge
  • Satin Inferno Burst finish
  • 9-volt clip-in battery compartment
  • Right-handed 5-string configuration

This Riot-5 combines responsive playability, versatile active electronics, and refined aesthetics into a serious working instrument at a B-stock price point.

This specific B-Stock unit features minor cosmetic imperfections typical of warehouse inventory exposure. These marks are purely superficial and have absolutely zero impact on the playability, tone, or structural integrity of the bass. Acquire this premium Schecter Riot-5 P/J configuration at a significant B-Stock discount while maintaining complete manufacturer warranty coverage.

item.title: 1985 Fender Precision Bass ‘62 Vintage Reissue PB62-55 Metallic Pink Japan MIJ Fujigen — $2,542.52
item.description:
$2,542.52 · Good · Lucky cat Guitar market(JAPAN)

This is a Fender Japan PB62-55 Precision Bass. Based on the A-prefix serial number and the 1985 neck date, this bass was made in 1985. It features a rare Metallic Pink finish, which was not a standard catalog color. Made by Fujigen, this is a very hard-to-find color even in Japan, so don’t miss this opportunity if you have been looking for one.

Body: Basswood
Neck: Maple
Fingerboard: Rosewood

Weight: approx. 4.0 kg

As this bass is around 40 years old, it shows signs of use appropriate for its age. There are several noticeable scratches, as well as smaller scratches and some aging on the hardware/parts. There is some fret wear, but there are currently no dead notes or playability issues. The electronics are most likely original and function properly.

The included accessory is a Fender hardshell case. It is a very tight fit, so the bass needs to be pressed in slightly to fit properly.



Please note that this is a used item.
There are no issues with its current functionality, but please understand that you may need to make further adjustments after purchase to suit your preferences.
Be sure to review the photos and description carefully before placing a bid.
If there’s anything you’d like to confirm, please don’t hesitate to contact us.


Important Notice

Payments are accepted via PayPal only.

Please ensure the shipping address in your PayPal account exactly matches the one registered on Reverb.

We will ship to the Reverb-registered address even if you use Guest Checkout or if the PayPal address does not match. However, PayPal Buyer/Seller Protection may not apply in such cases.

PayPal transaction fees are non-refundable on any cancellation.


Customs and Import Regulations

We do not undervalue items or mark them as “gifts,” as this is prohibited by regulations in Japan, the United States, and other countries.

Import duties, taxes, and any other charges are the buyer’s responsibility. Please check with your local customs office in advance.

In rare cases, especially for high-value items, U.S. Customs or the carrier may request the buyer’s Tax Identification Number (such as an SSN or CBP Form 5106) for clearance. This is a standard procedure, and failure to provide it may result in delays or the return of the package.


Thank you for your understanding!


Management Number: F-2626

item.title: Schecter SLS ELITE-5 Electric Bass in Black Fade Burst — $1,649
item.description:
$1,649 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Schecter SLS ELITE-5 Electric Bass in Black Fade Burst

The SLS ELITE-5 delivers a balanced, comfortable playing experience with its thin C-profile neck and 35-inch scale, designed for extended reach and articulate low-end definition. Neck-thru construction with Ultra Access combines structural integrity with practical fretboard accessibility, while the swamp ash body with flamed maple top produces warmth and clarity across the frequency spectrum. This is a serious instrument for players who demand active electronics, versatile voicing options, and meticulous hardware.

Playability & Build

The maple/walnut/padauk multi-ply neck with carbon fiber reinforcement rods ensures rock-solid stability and minimal neck dive, while the ebony fretboard with 24 X-Jumbo stainless steel frets supports both aggressive playing and precise intonation. Arched top contour and ivory binding enhance both visual appeal and ergonomic comfort, and the 16-inch fretboard radius accommodates modern playing techniques across the entire range.

Tone & Electronics

Dual Fishman Fluence Bass Soapbar pickups paired with a 3-way voicing switch (Classic, Funk, Modern) deliver tonal flexibility from traditional warm bass to cutting modern clarity, while the 2-band active EQ and master volume with push-pull single-coil mode provide real-time shaping. The Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut and Grover Bass 144 Series tuners ensure excellent sustain and tuning stability.

Key Features
  • Neck-Thru construction with Ultra Access
  • 35-inch scale length
  • Swamp ash body with flamed maple top
  • Maple/walnut/padauk multi-ply neck with carbon fiber reinforcement rods
  • Ebony fretboard with 24 X-Jumbo stainless steel frets
  • Thin C neck contour; 16-inch fretboard radius
  • Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut (45mm)
  • Dual Fishman Fluence Bass Soapbar pickups
  • 3-way toggle voicing switch (Classic, Funk, Modern)
  • 2-band active Fishman EQ with master volume and push-pull single-coil switch
  • Grover Bass 144 Series tuners
  • Schecter custom string-thru bridge with black hardware

The SLS ELITE-5 combines premium construction materials, modern active electronics, and thoughtful ergonomic design into a versatile 5-string platform suitable for studio work and performance across multiple musical contexts.

item.description:

The CV-5 delivers a comfortable, responsive playing experience with its thin 'C' neck profile and extended 35" scale, designed to accommodate five-string technique without fatigue. Playability & Neck Construction

The maple neck with carbon fiber reinforcement rods ensures long-term stability and resistance to seasonal fluctuation, while the thin 'C' shape (measuring .787" at the first fret and .905" at the 12th) provides quick access to the fingerboard. A 10" fretboard radius with 22 narrow X-jumbo frets balances comfort with precision, and the Graph Tech XL Ivory Tusq nut at 1.771" width accommodates the five-string spacing with clarity and sustain.

Tone & Electronics

Dual Schecter Diamond J-Bucker pickups paired with independent 3-way series-split-parallel pickup switches deliver flexibility from focused single-coil character to blended fullness. The control layout—volume, tone, 3-way switch, and dual pickup configurators with metal knurled knobs—grants intuitive access to a wide palette of timbres across genres.

Key Features

  • 2-way adjustable truss rod with heel access

The Schecter CV-5 combines stable construction, responsive playability, and versatile pickup switching to deliver a professional-level five-string instrument ready for immediate use.

item.title: Sire MARCUS MILLER V5 Alder 4 TS Tobacco Sunburst / Jazz Bass Type / 2nd Generation / 2N22210681 — $475

item.title: Schecter Stiletto Studio-5 FL Electric Bass Honey Satin — $1,299
item.description:

Schecter Stiletto Studio-5 FL Electric Bass Honey Satin

The Stiletto Studio-5 FL delivers a compact, responsive playing experience through its thin 'C' neck profile and 35" extended scale, engineered for precise intonation and articulate fretless technique. The neck-through construction with Ultra Access body contour provides exceptional upper-fret access and tonal sustain, while the Honey Satin finish over mahogany and bubinga presents a refined aesthetic. Playability & Construction

The thin 'C' neck measures .787" at the first fret and .866" at the 12th, paired with a 16" fretboard radius for comfortable lateral movement and intonation accuracy on the fretless rosewood board. Tone & Electronics

Dual EMG 40HZ pickups with a 3-band active EQ and master volume/blend control deliver articulate, punchy response across the frequency spectrum—ideal for cutting through in a mix or achieving vocal-like expression. The 18-volt active system with dual EMG configurations allows for aggressive tone shaping and extended dynamic range necessary for modern fretless work.

item.title: Ibanez SR895PD Standard Soundgear Bass — $899
item.description:
$899 · Very Good · SKR Guitars

1992 Ibanez SR895PD Bass – Made in Japan – EMG Upgraded – Includes Gig Bag

Offered for sale is a 1992 Ibanez SR895PD, a premium FujiGen-built Soundgear bass from the golden era of Japanese Ibanez production. These early SR basses are highly regarded for their exceptional craftsmanship, incredibly comfortable slim necks, lightweight ergonomic design, and versatile active electronics, making them favorites among both touring musicians and studio players. (ibanez.com⁠)

Finished in the beautiful Padauk (PD) natural finish, this bass features an ash body with a striking padauk top, a fast 3-piece maple neck, rosewood fingerboard, gold hardware, and the original Accu-Cast bridge. The combination of premium tonewoods provides outstanding sustain, excellent note definition, and the articulate, punchy response these early Japanese Soundgear basses are known for.

The original neck pickup has been upgraded to an EMG P-style pickup while the original Ibanez AFR bridge pickup remains, creating an excellent blend of modern punch and classic Ibanez character. The bass delivers deep, authoritative lows, clear mids, and articulate highs that work equally well for rock, funk, gospel, fusion, jazz, or studio recording.

The bass is in excellent playing condition with a straight neck, comfortable low action, and frets with plenty of life remaining. All electronics function as they should. Cosmetically, it shows normal signs of use for a 34-year-old instrument, including a few light dings and finish wear consistent with age. Overall, it has been very well cared for and presents beautifully.

A quality gig bag is included with the sale.

Please review all photos carefully for the condition of the instrument. Feel free to reach out with any questions or requests for additional photos—I’m happy to help! This item is sold as described and will be professionally packed and shipped using Reverb Safe Shipping for your peace of mind. Thank you for considering this listing!

Included with a hard case, the Gnarwhal Bass in Peach Fuzz is ready for the road and beyond. Stand out, sound fierce, and get your hands on the ultimate fusion of beauty and power with the Gnarwhal Bass in Peach Fuzz.

item.title: Fender 75th Anniversary Player II Precision Bass® - Aztec Gold(Pre-Order) — $1,013.31
item.description:
$1,013.31 · Brand New · Zjon's Guitarstore

Fender 75th Anniversary Player II Precision Bass® - Aztec Gold(Pre-Order)

ETA:Mid July 2026

Step into the spotlight and experience iconic Fender® sound and style with the Player II Precision Bass® — a stage-ready bass guitar with contemporary updates to power your performance and inspire your playing.

The Player II Precision Bass radiates timeless Fender charm, but under the hood, it's primed for today's players. Everything about the neck is designed for fast and fluid playability, from the Modern “C”-profile with silky satin urethane finish on the back to the comfy 9.5”-radius slab rosewood or maple fingerboard with smooth rolled edges and 20 medium jumbo frets. A classic alder body is available in both timeless Fender finishes and never-before-seen colors unearthed from the archives. Player Series Alnico V Split-Coil P bass® pickups offer sweet highs, musical mids and seismic lows that elevate any genre. An adjustable 4-saddle bridge, single-groove steel saddles and open-gear tuners ensure precise tuning stability for the flexibility to explore endless sonic possibilities.

Perfect for crafting your own sound, the Player II Precision Bass has the looks, tone and feel that only a Fender delivers.

FEATURES

-75th Anniversary Limited Colors
-75th Anniversary Neck Plate
-Contoured Alder Body
-Modern "C" Neck Profile
-9.5“-Radius Maple or Rosewood Fingerboard with Rolled Edges
-Player Series Alnico V Split-Coil Precision Bass® Pickups

HIGHLIGHTS

CONTOURED ALDER BODY
Accept no substitutes! When you want authentic Fender tone, you must start with authentic Fender tonewood. Our iconic contoured alder bodies lay the perfect foundation for textbook Fender tone.
MODERN "C" NECK PROFILE
This neck is designed for supremely comfortable performance, with silky smooth back finish and our Modern “C”-profile — for first-class feel that’s ideal for almost any playing style.
9.5“-RADIUS MAPLE OR ROSEWOOD FINGERBOARD WITH ROLLED EDGES
The 9.5”-radius fingerboard provides fast fretting and easy playability, while the hand-rolled fingerboard edges provide a slightly broken-in feeling that’s incredibly inviting.
PLAYER SERIES PICKUPS
A modern tweak of our classic sound, our Player Series pickups are designed with more power and more bite, for authentic Fender tone—with a bit of an edge.
ADJUSTABLE 4-SADDLE BRIDGE WITH SINGLE-GROOVE STEEL "BARREL" SADDLES
A vintage-style bass bridge with single-groove saddles provides classic Fender tone with precise intonation and rock-solid stability.
OPEN-GEAR TUNING MACHINES
Featuring a modern gear ratio and direction, these open-gear tuning machines combine vintage style with modern performance for rock-solid tuning stability.

Specs:

Body
BODY:Alder
BODY FINISH:Polyester
BODY SHAPE:Precision Bass®
BODY STYLE:Solid Body
BODY MATERIAL:Alder

General
SERIES:Limited Edition 75th Anniversary Precision Bass Collection

COLOR:Aztec Gold

item.title: Fender 75th Anniversary Player II Precision Bass® - Daphne Blue(Pre-Order) — $1,013.31
item.description:
$1,013.31 · Brand New · Zjon's Guitarstore

Fender 75th Anniversary Player II Precision Bass® - Daphne Blue(Pre-Order)

ETA:Mid July 2026

Step into the spotlight and experience iconic Fender® sound and style with the Player II Precision Bass® — a stage-ready bass guitar with contemporary updates to power your performance and inspire your playing.

The Player II Precision Bass radiates timeless Fender charm, but under the hood, it's primed for today's players. Everything about the neck is designed for fast and fluid playability, from the Modern “C”-profile with silky satin urethane finish on the back to the comfy 9.5”-radius slab rosewood or maple fingerboard with smooth rolled edges and 20 medium jumbo frets. A classic alder body is available in both timeless Fender finishes and never-before-seen colors unearthed from the archives. Player Series Alnico V Split-Coil P bass® pickups offer sweet highs, musical mids and seismic lows that elevate any genre. An adjustable 4-saddle bridge, single-groove steel saddles and open-gear tuners ensure precise tuning stability for the flexibility to explore endless sonic possibilities.

Perfect for crafting your own sound, the Player II Precision Bass has the looks, tone and feel that only a Fender delivers.

FEATURES

-75th Anniversary Limited Colors
-75th Anniversary Neck Plate
-Contoured Alder Body
-Modern "C" Neck Profile
-9.5“-Radius Maple or Rosewood Fingerboard with Rolled Edges
-Player Series Alnico V Split-Coil Precision Bass® Pickups

HIGHLIGHTS

CONTOURED ALDER BODY
Accept no substitutes! When you want authentic Fender tone, you must start with authentic Fender tonewood. Our iconic contoured alder bodies lay the perfect foundation for textbook Fender tone.
MODERN "C" NECK PROFILE
This neck is designed for supremely comfortable performance, with silky smooth back finish and our Modern “C”-profile — for first-class feel that’s ideal for almost any playing style.
9.5“-RADIUS MAPLE OR ROSEWOOD FINGERBOARD WITH ROLLED EDGES
The 9.5”-radius fingerboard provides fast fretting and easy playability, while the hand-rolled fingerboard edges provide a slightly broken-in feeling that’s incredibly inviting.
PLAYER SERIES PICKUPS
A modern tweak of our classic sound, our Player Series pickups are designed with more power and more bite, for authentic Fender tone—with a bit of an edge.
ADJUSTABLE 4-SADDLE BRIDGE WITH SINGLE-GROOVE STEEL "BARREL" SADDLES
A vintage-style bass bridge with single-groove saddles provides classic Fender tone with precise intonation and rock-solid stability.
OPEN-GEAR TUNING MACHINES
Featuring a modern gear ratio and direction, these open-gear tuning machines combine vintage style with modern performance for rock-solid tuning stability.

item.title: 1980 Yamaha BB-VI Broad Bass VI MIJ Japan Vintage Electric Bass Black — $1,299.99
item.description:
$1,299.99 · Very Good · Tone House

1980 Yamaha BB-VI Broad Bass VI MIJ Japan Vintage Electric Bass Black

1980 Yamaha Broad Bass VI (BB-VI) Vintage Electric Bass – Made in Japan

Offered here is a 1980 Yamaha Broad Bass VI (BB-VI), one of Yamaha's most respected vintage basses from the highly regarded Broad Bass series. Built in Japan, the BB-VI earned its reputation for exceptional craftsmanship, outstanding reliability, and a powerful, punchy tone that has made these instruments favorites among players and collectors alike.

Inspired by the classic Precision Bass design while incorporating Yamaha's own engineering and refinements, the BB-VI delivers a thick, articulate low end with excellent sustain and clarity. Its solid alder body, comfortable maple neck, and passive split-coil pickup make it equally at home in rock, blues, funk, soul, and countless other musical styles.

Specifications

  • Model: Broad Bass VI (BB-VI)
  • Year: 1980
  • Serial Number: 300553
  • Country of Origin: Japan
  • Body: Alder
  • Neck: Bolt-On Maple
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood
  • Frets: 21
  • Scale Length: 34"
  • Nut Width: 1.70"
  • Weight: 9 lbs 10 oz

Electronics

  • Yamaha Split-Coil Precision-Style Pickup
  • Master Volume
  • Master Tone
  • Passive Electronics

Condition

This bass is in very good overall condition, especially considering its age. It shows honest cosmetic wear throughout, including a handful of dings and dents consistent with over four decades of use.

The sixth fret has a small divot that does not affect playability or performance. The bass has been professionally set up with smooth, low playing action and is fully functional.

A fantastic opportunity to own a vintage Made-in-Japan Yamaha Broad Bass, celebrated for its outstanding build quality, classic styling, and powerful vintage tone.



  • At Tone House, we're not just sellers, we're passionate musicians, gear aficionados, and seasoned professionals dedicated to the craft of making music. We take pride in our curated music collection. We love giving these vintage and rare pieces new life through maintenance and upkeep. Our goal is for these units to be at their optimal performance before going to their new homes.  Every item you acquire from us undergoes a meticulous inspection process conducted by our team of enthusiasts, studio engineers, and live performers. With full-time technicians on staff, rest assured that each piece is thoroughly examined and tested before being professionally packed.

All orders placed by 2 pm are processed the same day. This ensures that your gear is swiftly prepared for shipment, guaranteeing prompt delivery in the condition promised. Once your order is in transit, automatic tracking information is provided, allowing you to monitor its journey from our shop to your doorstep with ease.

The units/items you see in our pictures are precisely what you'll receive. Have questions? Feel free to ask before making a purchase. Additionally, we offer custom modifications, within reason, to many of the guitars and products we sell, ensuring they perfectly suit your unique requirements.

Our dedication to your satisfaction doesn't end with the purchase. We value your feedback and strive for excellence in every transaction. If, for any reason, you're not completely satisfied, please reach out to us before leaving feedback. Your happiness is our priority, and we're here to ensure your musical journey is nothing short of exceptional.


*BUY WITH CONFIDENCE*

Please note that additional shipping charges may apply to buyers in Hawaii, Alaska, Puerto Rico, and the Virgin Islands. Feel free to contact us for shipping rates before making your purchase.

For Returns: Your satisfaction is our priority. If for any reason you're not completely satisfied with your purchase, please don't hesitate to reach out to us via message. We're here to address any concerns and ensure you have a positive experience with Tone House.

item.title: Fender American Deluxe Precision Bass 3-Colour Sunburst 2012 Electric Bass — $1,867.48
item.description:
$1,867.48 · Very Good · Sell Us Your Guitar

Here for sale we have a Fender American Deluxe Precision Bass in 3-Colour Sunburst finish. Made in the USA in 2012, this bass consists of a Select Alder body, a Maple neck, and a 21-fret Rosewood fingerboard. Other appointments include a High Mass Vintage bridge, Chrome hardware, a Stealth A string retainer, rolled fingerboard edges, a Bone nut, and Fender tuning machines with an additional Fender/Hipshot D-Tuner. The pickups in this bass are a Deluxe Split Single-Coil Precision Bass Pickup, and a N3 Noiseless Jazz Bass Pickup with controls for master volume, a pan pot, 3-band EQ, and an active/passive mini toggle switch.


The Maple neck plays very comfortably, with Fenders Modern ‘C’ profile filling the hand in an assuring way while still allowing for comfortable reach in all registers and promoting ergonomic thumb positioning. The double cutaway body design offers extended access into the upper register. This works well with the satin finish to the rear of the neck, allowing for smooth navigation of the 34” scale. The Medium Jumbo fret wire combined with the 9.5” radius makes for a great feel under the fingers, and provides the versatility required for a wide range of styles and techniques


This bass is equipped with a Deluxe Split Single-Coil Precision Bass Pickup and a N3 Noiseless Jazz Bass Pickup in a PJ configuration. This is a versatile combination which delivers a deep and powerful tone; the best bits of Fender's most iconic bass guitars.  They provide plenty of depth, warmth and a rounded low end that works very well with the clear, precise mids and glistening trebles. There is plenty of grunt on offer, with a fast attack that will keep your playing front and centre. The comprehensive controls offer the ability to explore every tonal option in active or passive mode, giving the player endless options.


Brand- Fender

Model- American Deluxe Precision Bass

Finish- 3-Colour Sunburst

Year- 2012

Made In- USA

Body- Select Alder

Neck- Maple

Neck Profile- Modern 'C'

Fingerboard- Rosewood

Fingerboard Radius- 9.5"

Frets- 21, Medium

Scale Length- 34"

Nut Width- 1.631"

Pickups- 1x Deluxe Split Single-Coil Precision Bass Pickup, 1x N3 Noiseless Jazz Bass Pickup

Controls- Master Volume, Pan Pot, 3-band Active EQ, Active/Passive Mini-Toggle Switch


Luthier Report


Our ID: 1220626218020 

Weight: 9.45lbs 

Serial Number: US11279954 


Aesthetic Condition:

Headstock: Overall excellent condition, the top rear edge demonstrates some light indentation which does not pierce the finish. Light scratching is present to the front face of the headstock, these can only be seen under direct light, the most apparent of these can be seen to the treble side below the Made In USA logo.

Neck: Overall excellent condition, the fingerboard demonstrates very light playwear throughout, difficult to discern from the natural grain. Very light indentations are present to the treble side rear of the neck, these can be seen under close scrutiny at the 11th fret and can be slightly felt in the playing position. 

Body: Overall very good condition, the rear face demonstrates light indentation to the center of the face visible only under direct lighting, consistent with buckle rash. All aspects of the body demonstrate very light scratching and indentation visible only under direct lighting at certain angles. 


Condition of Frets:

The frets demonstrate light signs of use, with slight indentation in the early register that does not affect playability. There is considerable material remaining for future fret work. 


Current Set Up:

Truss Rod: Fully functional. Truss rod tool (3/16" hex) included.

Hardware: Overall very good condition and fully functional. The original tuning machines have been swapped with a set of Fender branded machines with an addition of a  Fender/Hipshot De-tuner for the low E tuning machine, the original tuning machines are not included. The original pickguard has been previously changed to a tortoiseshell pick guard, the original is not included. Light to mild tarnishing can be seen to all aspects of the metal hardware, this is most apparent to the bridge.

Structural: No concerns or issues to note. 

Strings on Guitar - 45-100

Height of string from 12th Fret- Treble 2mm - Bass 2.5mm

Please be aware that the action on a guitar may move slightly in transit and this measurement is the current height. 


Electrics: 

Fully functional.


Case & Accessories:

No case is provided with this guitar.

Original Fender paperwork, cleaning cloth and Fender truss rod tool included.


Please be aware that for international buyers import fees are not included in our postage and packaging cost, this only covers the cost of the guitar being sent to you. Import fees will be charged by Customs upon the guitar's arrival in the recipient country. Due to Brexit, this also applies to EU buyers. We advise checking the total import cost with Customs as this is not information we are able to accurately provide.

item.title: Fender Precision Bass with rosewood fretboard 1962 — $17,969.26
item.description:
$17,969.26 · Very Good · Wax Musical Ltd

Relisted due to mistaken order.

This bass has been played/owned from 1962-2024 by George Jones of the Warrington Renegades.

They supported the Beatles in 1962, plus the other bands below:

Rolling Stones 1963

Spencer Davis Group (with Winwood)

Long John Baldry & Steam packet with:

Rod Stewart

Elton John

----------------------

Since the Renegades were simply a local support act it is hard to provide evidence but I will provide a COA that is belonged to George.

Any questions please ask.

-----------------------

NOW READY TO BE SOLD AND SHIPPED ANYWHERE IN THE WORLD WITH A BRAND NEW PRECISION BASS RIGID FOAM POD CASE.

This bass has now had two professional setups. I think it plays and sounds great, but others may prefer to set up the bass with a bridge adjustment. The buyer is free to choose, natch!

What we can be sure of is this: the neck is fine, with truss rod working perfectly.

The bridge is original yet in great condition with plenty of play to adjust also.

The instrument is original in all regards apart from one pot (volume) which has been replaced with a modern pot that functions perfectly.

I am more than happy to have all this work carried out free of charge before the instrument is shipped to any buyer. AFTER PURCHASE.

Part exchange is possible in the UK.

I am serious about selling this historic P-Bass before the end of the month. It is under-priced - I know that - and I appreciate concerns but I will not respond to any more messages telling me it is too cheap! The fact is, we sell instruments below their market price - that is how we function. It may not surprise you to learn i only employ one member of staff and I drive a Nissan Note!

--------------

MORE INFO

- Fret wear is low due to it having been played with round wounds its entire life

- Pot codes on tone pot date it early 1962

- I will produce bill of sale & accompanying booklet with copy pics, letters, & COA to ship with the instrument.

We survive by selling excellent gear below market values. Check our previous listings and feedback.

We send worldwide every week, safely.

Genuine 1962 Fender Precision bass with the desirable “slab” neck. All completely original parts aside from one potentiometer. Original “sunburst” finish with a beautiful authentic patina – this is an old, well-loved instrument and it looks the part. Not only that, but it sounds just as great as you’d expect an instrument of this pedigree to sound, even 64 years later: You can see a video of it being played below.

The instrument also has a remarkable claim to fame. We can directly trace its provenance to George Jones, bass player in a Warrington band called The Renegades. The Renegades played several notable gigs locally, most importantly acting as support act to The Beatles at their Bell Hall concert in July 1962, and to the Rolling Stones at Parr Hall in November 1963.

The guitar

· 1962 Fender Precision bass

· 'slab' style neck with ceramic dot markers and side-markers

· serial number on the neck plate

· original hand-wound Fender pickups, wound by Pilar Lopez (one of the original Fender pickup winders, who learned directly from Leo Fender). This is evidenced by her signature on the metal under-scratch plate!

· idiosyncratic body shape and depth

· original un-refinished 'sunburst' finish

· authentic headstock layout

· original tuners

· one replaced potentiometer; the other is original

· minor crack on the back of headstock from a tuner screw; purely cosmetic and not affecting neck strength, filled and stabilised by pro guitar tech.

We are offering this exciting and unique instrument for sale worldwide through Reverb. Generally you would only be able to get your hands on something of this calibre by going through one of the big auction houses such as Sotheby’s or Christie’s, but selling direct through Reverb has allowed us to offer this at a lower price point than its estimated value.

The bass can be viewed and demo’ed by appointment at our store in Chorley, Lancs.

Final update: This instrument is now ready for serious sale and we can make the following statements having had the instrument checked by a number of experts...

1. This is a genuine 1962 Fender Precision with the desirable 'slab' neck

2. The body, the sunburst finish, the neck finish, the tuners, the neck plate, the scratchplate and aluminium under-plate are all original.

3. This guitar was owned - without a doubt - by the Bassist George Jones who played with the Renegades (Warrington). That band supported the Beatles at Bell Hall in Warrington in 1962, and the Rolling Stones at Parr Hall Warrington in 1963. These gigs are well documented, as are the Renegades appearances there. At Bell Hall the Renegades' drummer had to use Pete Best's kit due to having another gig the same evening. This was one of Pete Best's final appearances with the Beatles.

UPDATE 2: ALL CONFIRMED - ORIGINAL SLAB NECK EST PRODUCTION DATE FOR BASS FEB 1962

UPDATE 1: This precision bass does has 1 original pot and the pickups are original.

This historic bass is priced below the lowest estimate that professionals have given for an instrument with this background and provenance. This is because we are selling thru Reverb and not Christies, Sotheby's or any of the big auction houses.

The estimate we were given was £20k - £30k for a '62 precision with this history.

This is a 1962 Fender Precision bass, which has the 'slab' style neck with ceramic dot markers and side-markers, the serial number on the neck plate, the signature 'Pilar' (Pilar Lopez, one of the original Fender pickup winders) on the metal 'under-scratch plate', the idiosyncratic body shape and depth, and the original un-refinished 'sunburst' finish,

The very genuine relic appearance, the layout of the headstock, the tuners, the fact that it was bought from a relative of George Jones (bass player of the Renegades) and the photo of him with the instrument in the very early 1960's all testify to the veracity of the fact that this bass guitar was used to support the Beatles on their first Warrington show.

We acquired this bass in the town of Warrington - where the Beatles played in the early 60s (see poster).

It was purchased from a relative of George Jones himself.

George Jones played bass on that show with the Renegades (again, see the pictures) and played bass with the Renegades to support the Rolling Stones on their Warrington show the year afterwards.

The bass still plays and sounds amazing. On the video shown I (Sam Dooley) am playing it with old flats thru a tinny practice guitar amp. It really does not do it justice - when we complete a set-up and play it thru a good amp we will do another video (if we still have it).

I have a whole lot more info and photos but have decided to get the listing up and running. I've received an email from Fender saying they think it is a great find. The email text is below.

We will provide a COA of when, where and how we acquired it and how the history can be supported.

There are no date stamps on the neck that I can see, but Fender say they often got rubbed off. The facts are; the serial number checks out for early '62 and everything else on the guitar is absolutely commensurate with a '62 P-Bass - include the history of the ownership.

This is a historic instrument with a great provenance and is very possibly worth much more than we are asking. It is available to play by appointment at our store in Lancs, and it plays very well indeed. I will ensure there is a proper bass amp available if this is arranged!

EMAIL FROM FENDER TO SUPPORT AUTHENTICITY HERE:

Cedric (Fender Musical Instruments)

30 Apr 2026, 16:54 BST

Dear Sam,

Thank you for reaching out and for the fascinating details you've shared about this Precision Bass. What a wonderful find — potentially a very significant piece of British beat-era music history if the provenance can be confirmed. Let me share what I can from our side.

Serial number 75733 — plausible for 1962

Your serial number 75733 is indeed consistent with 1962 production. Early Fender serial numbers were stamped on the neck plate in rough chronological batches:

Year Approximate serial range

1961 55000 – 72000

1962

72000 – 93000

1963 93000 – L10000

So 75733 would sit comfortably in early-to-mid 1962. However, we have to be transparent: neck plate serial numbers from that era are not a reliable dating tool on their own. Neck plates were produced in batches and used as needed, sometimes out of strict sequence, and plates can be swapped during repair or restoration. The serial confirms plausibility, not certainty.

About the missing date stamps

Given that you've already removed and inspected the neck pocket and neck heel without finding any date markings, the most likely explanations are:

The body has been refinished at some point, which can obscure or remove pencil marks in the neck pocket

The neck or body has been replaced during its working life (very common on gigging instruments of that era)

The pencil marks have simply faded away — they're not ink, just soft pencil on raw wood, and can disappear over 60+ years of handling and exposure

In the absence of neck/body date stamps, the most reliable remaining dating evidence will come from:

Pot codes on the potentiometers (e.g., a code such as "304 6220" would indicate CTS, week 20 of 1962)

Pickup date — sometimes written or stamped on the underside of the pickup

Period-correct hardware and components — tuners, bridge, pickguard screws, wiring, etc.

The "Pilar" signature

The signature "Pilar" on the underside of the pickguard is a genuinely encouraging sign. Fender's Fullerton factory in the early 1960s had a small team of assembly workers, and several of them — Tadeo Gomez, Virginia, Mary, Gloria, and yes, Pilar — frequently signed or initialled the components they assembled. These signatures are considered markers of authenticity and are well-documented in Fender collector circles. Finding "Pilar" on a pickguard from a 1962 P-Bass is entirely consistent and a very good sign.

The George Jones / Renegades provenance

Regarding the claim that this bass was owned by George Jones of The Renegades, who supported The Beatles in Liverpool — we unfortunately cannot verify or authenticate personal ownership history from our side. Fender does not hold ownership records for vintage instruments, and provenance claims of this nature can only be substantiated by:

Documentary evidence — original bills of sale, photographs of the musician playing the instrument, gig posters, tour programmes, letters

Chain of custody — a traceable line of previous owners with supporting paperwork

Testimony — statements from the musician, their family, bandmates, or estate

Photographic matching — period photographs where unique features of the bass (grain patterns, wear marks, scratches) can be matched to the instrument you now hold

If genuine, that is a tremendously valuable piece of Merseybeat/British Invasion history. The Renegades were indeed active in the early 1960s and had Liverpool connections, so the story is at least historically plausible — but plausible is not proven. We'd strongly encourage you to gather as much documentary evidence as possible from whoever you acquired it from.

What we can confirm from our side

Based on everything you've shared, what we can say with reasonable confidence from our end is:

The serial number 75733 is period-correct for a 1962 Precision Bass

The "Pilar" signature on the pickguard is consistent with genuine Fullerton-era factory assembly

The overall description is consistent with a genuine pre-CBS Fender Precision Bass
